place: Belleplain CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Belleplain got its name when the West Jersey Railroad superintendent honored Annabelle Wilson Townsend Lee, the beautiful daughter of William S.
Early industry in Belleplain included a brick factory that could fire 100,000 bricks at once and a sawmill that supplied lumber to the region.
A former cranberry bog was turned into Lake Nummy during a Civilian Conservation Corps project in the Franklin Roosevelt era.
Belleplain’s historic Hands Mill sits just beyond the Dennis Township line and still echoes the town’s early industrial past.
